title: Reviews Data and Schedules Public Checklist

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7 and §8 review discipline, in your milestone reviews:
records.query: Have you planned the key reviews — Requirements Review, Preliminary Design Review, Critical Design Review, and Test Readiness Review?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §7 and §8 review discipline, in your milestone reviews:
records.query: At each review, do you confirm the right data is ready, and capture issues and track them to closure?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2 planning, does your project plan:
records.query: Show the major software milestones and how the life-cycle phases transition?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2 planning, does your project plan:
records.query: Plan certification liaison — when and what you will submit to the FAA?

records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §11.2 planning, does your project plan:
records.query: Identify the people, tools, and facilities (the resources) needed to do the work?

records.section: Section 3 — General Software Data and Delivery
records.query: Is the data complete, controlled, and clearly identified, so a reviewer can find what they need?

records.section: Section 3 — General Software Data and Delivery
records.query: Is the data marked with the right status and delivered in the agreed form?

records.section: Section 4 — Previously Developed and COTS Software
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.1, if you reuse software:
records.query: Have you shown reused or COTS software meets the objectives for your software level — not just that it worked before?

records.section: Section 4 — Previously Developed and COTS Software
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.1, if you reuse software:
records.query: Have you assessed the impact of any modification to it, or of using it in a changed environment?

records.section: Section 4 — Previously Developed and COTS Software
records.subsection: Based on DO-178B/C §12.1, if you reuse software:
records.query: If you rely on product service history, do you have the data to support that argument (relevant environment, enough hours, problem records)?
notes: This free checklist is a high-level sanity check — free to use and share. It doesn't include the item-level actions needed to fully close each objective; those live in AeroCert's comprehensive checklist, kept current as the standards change and available through donations. We'd love to hear how it worked for you — tell us at support@aerocert.org.
